scenario: Project Freedom launched May 4: US CENTCOM deploys guided-missile destroyers, 100+ aircraft, 15,000 service members to Hormuz to 'restore freedom of navigation' (NBC, PBS, Axios). Iran warns ceasefire violation; FM reviewing 14-point proposal. Brent $108.12 May 4 (0.0%, TradingEconomics; flat vs May 1 $108.10). TTF €45.08 (-2.3%, TradingEconomics). JKM carried $16.48 Apr 28. Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; Hormuz ~90% closed; tail risk 45%.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat eases slightly (May 4 est.; Brent $108.12 flat, TradingEconomics); Project Freedom announced - potential partial Hormuz transit eases shipping premium modestly. Fertilizer supply disruption mechanism unchanged; Qafco force majeure persists. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation slightly softer with Brent $108.12 flat (May 4, TradingEconomics). Project Freedom announced - blockade and P&I suspension intact; fertilizer and Cape routing disruption mechanism unchanged. Iran warns Project Freedom is ceasefire violation - tail 45% intact.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs flat with Brent $108.12 (May 4, TradingEconomics); Project Freedom announced - Cape routing costs persist; blockade and P&I cancellation intact; structural supply chain disruption unresolved; tail 45%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO flat with Brent $108.12 (May 4, TradingEconomics); Project Freedom slight de-escalation signal; tail 45%. Indonesia B40 mandate provides structural floor. Malaysia energy emergency (June supply uncertain) adds domestic cost pressure. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting cycle unresolved. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure persists; Brent $108.12 May 4 (flat); Project Freedom does not resolve Qafco force majeure or Q2 planting urea disruption; blockade intact; tail 45%. Malaysia June supply gap risk remains.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha flat with Brent $108.12 (May 4, TradingEconomics); Project Freedom creates ambiguous signal - Gulf condensate interdiction unchanged; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; Pengerang RAPID feedstock exposure elevated; tail 45%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene flat with naphtha (May 4 est.); Project Freedom may ease some SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks if ships begin transiting - but Iran warns ceasefire violation; blockade unchanged; vessel interdiction not lifted; tail 45%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol slightly eases (May 4 est.; Brent $108.12 flat, TradingEconomics); Project Freedom announcement; US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure unresolved; blockade intact. China MTO complexes: feedstock pressure elevated. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea eases slightly (May 4 est.; Brent $108.12 flat, TradingEconomics); Project Freedom announced but Qafco force majeure persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; blockade intact. US sanctions waiver expiry (Apr 19) tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Tail 45%. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP eases slightly (May 4 est.; Brent $108.12 flat); Project Freedom does not immediately resolve Saudi Ma'aden shipping disruption; US Gulf supply stretched; blockade intact. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam remains acute; tail 45%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight costs ease slightly (May 4 est.; Brent $108.12 flat). Less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP - freight remains main transmission. Project Freedom may ease Cape rerouting premium modestly if ships transit; palm oil plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia face moderately elevated input costs.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI ticks up (May 4 est.) on Project Freedom signal - dry bulk reacts to partial transit hope; P&I suspension persists; blockade intact; mine-clearing 6 months even on deal. Iran warns ceasefire violation - normalization distant. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates firm on Project Freedom signal (May 4 est.) - transit expectation increases fixture demand; blockade intact; P&I suspension unresolved; Iran warns ceasefire violation - rate trajectory depends on whether US destroyers physically enable transits. Tail (45%) would push rates to cycle highs. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI carried (weekly; last published Friday May 1). Project Freedom announced but Cape rerouting premium persists - blockade intact, P&I unresolved. MSC Francesca still seized; Asia-Europe normalization requires Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration + mine-clearing (~6 months); tail 45%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly).
